phase_scope_assessment: Phase 1 is Roblox-safe as a scene scaffold and naming/hierarchy setup, but it is not yet a passable Roblox export package because the visible socket mesh has an unapplied rotation transform and no actual export artifact is produced.

render_reviewed.notes: The render confirms a simple visible WholeCell_ContextSocket scaffold only; no full nucleus export geometry is in scope for Phase 1.

component_names.notes: The two Phase 1 components use exact component_manifest.json names. Missing components are intentionally out of Phase 1 scope and are listed in the builder report.

export_001_transforms_are_applied_before.evidence: build_nucleus.py rotates WholeCell_ContextSocket after torus creation with rotation_euler[0] = 90 degrees, but does not call transform_apply before a potential mesh export.

export_002_origins_and_pivots_support_in.evidence: Nucleus_RootRig is an empty at world origin. WholeCell_ContextSocket is parented to it and retains a centered origin suitable for an alignment locator.

export_003_triangle_and_material_risks_a.evidence: Builder report lists future procedural material, transparency/emission, and mesh density risks. Current Phase 1 geometry is a single low-risk torus socket.

export_004_exported_component_names_are_.evidence: Component names are hard-coded and match the manifest without generated suffixes.

export_005_missing_manifest_components_a.evidence: Builder report lists every non-Phase-1 manifest component as incomplete.

root_rig.name: Nucleus_RootRig
root_rig.export_readiness: Suitable as hierarchy parent/animation pivot, but empties need explicit Roblox import handling or conversion to attachments/metadata.

context_socket.name: WholeCell_ContextSocket
context_socket.origin_signal: centered at socket location and parented to Nucleus_RootRig
context_socket.export_readiness: Object has an unapplied rotation transform and should not be exported to Roblox until transforms are applied or baked.

material_count_risk.notes: Only the teal context socket material is assigned in Phase 1. The script creates eight procedural Blender material recipes; later Roblox export will need baking or simplified material mapping because procedural nodes, alpha, emission, and bump do not transfer directly.

object_count_risk.notes: Object count is trivial in Phase 1. Future phases should keep repeated pores, chromatin strands, and transport cargo grouped into meaningful export components or instanced/LOD variants.

major_failures: WholeCell_ContextSocket has an unapplied 90-degree rotation transform in build_nucleus.py, so the current scene should not be treated as a final Roblox export pass.
minor_issues: No FBX, OBJ, glTF, or Roblox-ready exported artifact is produced in Phase 1; this is acceptable for scaffold scope but prevents end-to-end import validation.
Nucleus_RootRig is an empty, which is useful in Blender but needs an explicit Roblox-side representation plan such as a Model pivot, Attachment, or metadata marker.
The script creates eight procedural material recipes even though only one is used by current geometry; this is not a performance problem yet, but future export should avoid shipping unused materials.
Procedural noise, bump, transparency, and emission are Blender-specific and will require baking or Roblox material approximation in later phases.
required_fixes: Before any Roblox export, apply or bake transforms on WholeCell_ContextSocket and all future mesh components.
Define how Nucleus_RootRig and WholeCell_ContextSocket map into Roblox, including Model pivot, Attachment placement, and whether the socket is exported as visible geometry or setup-only metadata.
When Phase 2 introduces membrane meshes, record triangle counts, material counts, texture counts, object counts, and any LOD or simplification plan in the builder report.
Keep all future major objects named exactly from component_manifest.json and continue listing intentionally incomplete manifest components by phase.
manual_setup_notes: If imported before a dedicated exporter exists, exclude cameras and lights from the Roblox asset payload.
Treat Phase 1 as hierarchy/socket validation only, not as a nucleus mesh asset.
Bake or replace Blender procedural material features before Roblox import.
next_recommended_phase: Proceed to Phase 2 only as a modeling phase. Add the outer membrane, inner membrane, and perinuclear lumen as separate manifest-named components, and include transform-apply/export validation before marking Phase 2 export-ready.
